architecture.summary: TempoFit is a training-free temporal retrofit for frozen Vision-Language-Action policies that injects history through layer-wise K/V prefix caching. The system stores FIFO prefix K/V pairs at selected intermediate transformer layers across timesteps, retrieves relevant past context using Frame-Gap Temporal Bias (FGTB)—a fixed recency weighting inspired by NLP positional encodings—and reinjected via pre-attention residual loading with norm-preserving rescaling. No new tokens, no learnable modules, and no retraining of the VLA backbone; information flows entirely through the model's native attention state.

biblio.title: TempoFit: Plug-and-Play Layer-Wise Temporal KV Memory for Long-Horizon Vision-Language-Action Manipulation

claims.statement: TempoFit improves strong pretrained backbones by up to +4.0% average success rate on LIBERO-LONG
TempoFit maintains near-real-time latency while injecting temporal history
TempoFit transfers consistently to CALVIN and real-robot long-horizon tasks
Prefix attention K/V form a model-native, content-addressable runtime state that can be reused across timesteps without introducing new tokens or trainable modules
Frame-Gap Temporal Bias (FGTB) is a fixed recency bias inspired by positional biases in NLP that keeps decisions present-dominant
Pre-attention residual loading with norm-preserving rescaling avoids distribution shift under frozen weights
